actions-list.actionItem: The House proceeded with one hour of debate on H.R. 8029.

Rule provides for consideration of H.R. 8029, H. Res. 1128, H.R. 5103 and H.R. 7084. The resolution provides for consideration of H.R. 8029, H. Res. 1128, H.R. 5103, and H.R. 7084 under a closed rule with one hour of general debate on each measure. The resolution provides for one motion to recommit on H.R. 8029, H.R. 5103, and H.R. 7084.

Considered under the provisions of rule H. Res. 1131. H.R. 8029 — "Making appropriations for the Department of Homeland Security for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2026, and for other purposes."

At the conclusion of debate on H. Res. 1128, the Chair put the question on agreeing to the resolution and by voice vote, announced the ayes had prevailed. Mr. Thompson (MS) demanded the yeas and nays and the Chair postponed further proceedings until a time to be announced.
The previous question was ordered on the resolution and the preamble pursuant to the rule.
The House proceeded with one hour of debate on H. Res. 1128.

Rule provides for consideration of H.R. 8029, H. Res. 1128, H.R. 5103 and H.R. 7084. The resolution provides for consideration of H.R. 8029, H. Res. 1128, H.R. 5103, and H.R. 7084 under a closed rule with one hour of general debate on each measure. The resolution provides for one motion to recommit on H.R. 8029, H.R. 5103, and H.R. 7084.
Considered under the provisions of rule H. Res. 1131. H. Res. 1128 — "Expressing the support of the House of Representatives for the Department of Homeland Security."
The House proceeded with one minute speeches, which by direction of the Chair would be limited to 15 per side of the aisle.
The Chair designated Ms. Plaskett to lead the Members in reciting the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag.
The Speaker announced approval of the Journal.  Pursuant to clause 1, rule I, the Journal stands approved.
Today's prayer was offered by the House Chaplain, Chaplain Margaret Grun Kibben.
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of March 26.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is scheduled for 12:00 P.M. today.
The House proceeded with Morning-Hour Debate. At the conclusion of Morning-Hour, the House will recess until 12:00 p.m. for the start of legislative business.
The Speaker designated the Honorable Monica De La Cruz to act as Speaker pro tempore for today.
The House convened, starting a new legislative day.
